legal.intro: Muhandis is an open-source community for Uzbek engineers. This page explains what data we handle and why. We keep it to the minimum needed to run the network.
legal.collectBody: When you sign in with Telegram we receive your Telegram id, name, username, photo and language. We also store what you choose to add to your profile (bio, fields, skills, links) and the posts and comments you publish.
legal.useBody: To show you in the engineer directory, power the forum, send you Telegram notifications (comments, new followers) and connect you with other engineers. Nothing is required — you can stay anonymous.
legal.shareBody: We don't sell your data. Profile details you fill in are visible to the community as you choose. Authentication goes through Telegram and (optionally) Google. We use Neon (database) and Vercel (hosting) as infrastructure providers.
legal.deleteBody: You can edit or delete your posts and comments at any time. To delete your account and profile entirely, contact us via the Telegram community below.
legal.openSourceBody: The full source code is public under the MIT license, so anyone can inspect how data is handled or self-host their own instance.
legal.contactBody: Questions about privacy? Reach the team through the Muhandis Telegram community.

auth.miniAppOnly: Open inside Telegram, or use one of the methods below.
